title: elymus subvillosus
intro: Elymus subvillosus, also known as the hairy wheatgrass or hairy wildrye, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its hairy stems and panicles. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Elymus subvillosus is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing stress, improving digestion, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for its medicinal use, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies.
